creator: destination_wellknown
caption: Do NOT visit this island without checking the safe crossing times❗️  Holy Island in Northumberland surprised me so much on my latest trip to England. I’d expected a tiny island with an old castle, but I left with a sense of “I need to come back and stay here for a night or two.”   Lindisfarne Castle is the main attraction, and it’s just as cool in real life as in the photos, so I highly recommend stopping here if you’re in this part of England 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿  BUT you have to check the safe crossing times as the only road to the island gets flooded everyday 😅  There’s a great website that shows the safe (and unsafe) crossing times - on the day of my visit I had to leave before 2 pm, but as the moon decides the tide, it depends on the day you’re visiting.   Do a Google search for “Holy Island Crossing Times” and see the safe crossing times for the current month ✌🏻  Is this the coolest castle in England? I love places like this, so please share recommendations for other castles in England 🤝  #lindisfarne #holyisland #visitbritain #lovegreatbritain #northumberland #englandtravel #traveltips

transcription.text: Where are all these people going so it turns out that if you drive about an hour north from Newcastle You'll reach this island that was frequently raided by the Vikings And it's also home to one of the coolest castles that I've ever seen just make sure you leave the island while it's still low tide Or you'll be stuck there
